best_edges.evidence: pullback_long is the largest contributor: 31 trades, $2411.8 PnL, $252.1/hour, 100% win rate, no slow >=12h trades. Worst MAE is material at -403.2, but this is still the deepest proven edge by both PnL and efficiency.

breakout_long_1dte contributes 20 trades and $1305 PnL with no slow >=12h trades. Prior direct 1DTE tightening hurt score and net profit, so preserve the existing 1DTE breakout baseline while researching companion splits.
Only 5 trades, but $985.6 PnL, $197.1 average PnL, $234.4/hour, median MFE 146.2, no slow >=12h trades. Prior exit compression hurt, while admission geometry work recovered a broken branch.
32 trades and $1200.3 PnL with median hold 5.0 minutes and average MAE only -72.2, but it owns the global worst MAE -513.4. The edge is real, but tail repair must be profile/path-aware.
Only 1 trade, but it produced $91, $455/hour, worst MAE -11.7, median setup quality 72.5, and high ADX. This is not proven, but it is clean enough to justify isolated sample building.

broken_edges.evidence: breakout_long is routed but disabled and has 0 trades in the latest run. No evidence supports optimizing it before active edges are resolved.
broken_edges.problem: Dead inactive standard breakout variant with no sample.

broken_edges.evidence: wall_reclaim_long, wall_breakout_retest_long, late_day_pin_fade, negative_gamma_momentum_long, and trend_day_pullback_long all show 0 trades while disabled. Activating several at once would contaminate attribution.
broken_edges.problem: Dormant/inactive routed profiles with no current evidence.

broken_edges.evidence: STALE_0DTE fallback produced only 5 trades and $162.8 total PnL. breakout_long_no_0dte stale entries have weak efficiency: 2 trades, $71, $40.5/hour, median hold 52.6 minutes, avg MAE -210.6.
broken_edges.problem: No-0DTE stale fallback is positive but inefficient and sample-thin.

broken_edges.evidence: The prior 1dte-local-quality-gate reduced score by -594 and net profit by -$558.5. Current 1DTE edge remains profitable despite avg MAE -123.9 and worst MAE -375.7.
broken_edges.problem: Existing breakout_long_1dte should not be tightened directly; use companion split research instead.

broken_edges.evidence: Broad candidate filters such as RSI slope min 0 or ADX slope min 0 would retain only 73.8% and 60.0% PnL respectively and block many protected winners. Extended-VWAP winners total 18 trades and $695.3 with median MAE -14.9.
broken_edges.problem: Hidden MAE overlaps profitable fast/extended-VWAP winners, so broad entry pruning is dangerous.

market_regime_read: Short 12.3-day, high-win-rate, take-profit-only long scalper regime. Realized losses and slow >=12h trades are absent, but risk is hidden in MAE and long recovery paths: worst MAE -513.4, avg MAE about -101.5, only 9.5% of trades had MFE before MAE, and only 1.0% touched TP before MAE. The robust leader family around run_029/run_037/run_040 is stable near $7150 PnL, 107 trades, 0.4% drawdown, and -513.4 worst MAE. run_044 has higher net profit but worse MAE/drawdown, so the new-regime plan should discover profile/data-state splits that preserve run_029-style robustness while exploring call-wall, 1DTE companion, charm-wall, and charm-pin tail-state separation.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Path-dependent tail separation for charm-pin/pullback entries that recover only after deep MAE.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Overfits a handful of delayed winners and blocks genuine fast extended-VWAP scalps unless protected winner retention is enforced.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Early-Progress Heat Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Intratrade path samples: first +1.5/+2.0 point timing, first 15m MFE/MAE, MFE-before-MAE, TP-touch-before-MAE, profile, option data state.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: charm_pin_pullback_long or pullback_long entries that fail to make early positive progress, especially when MFE occurs after MAE and first +1.5 point is delayed.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Worst MAE improves toward <= -403 while retaining >=90% combined charm-pin/pullback PnL, >=95% protected fast winners, total net profit >=98% of robust leader, and slow_12h remains 0.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: 1DTE pullback/reclaim flow separated from breakout continuation.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Companion profile may cannibalize profitable breakout_long_1dte trades or be unreachable under current router behavior.
new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Profile
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_1DTE_ONLY trades, ES reclaim/pullback state, structure score, VWAP distance/slope, 1DTE option map availability, breakout_long_1dte retained trade attribution.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: 0DTE unavailable, 1DTE map valid, and ES state resembles pullback/reclaim rather than clean breakout continuation.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= $1305, 1DTE average MAE improves versus -123.9, breakout_long_1dte retained PnL is measured, total net profit >=98% of leader, and score flat or better.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Dormant pin-avoid split may isolate charm-wall/charm-pin tail states without damaging existing profiles.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Router activation may add chop, cannibalize charm_pin_pullback_long, or create no trades, making attribution weak.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Wall Pin-Avoid Dormant Router Probe
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_0DTE router attribution, charm wall/pin proximity, profile-level PnL, MAE, protected fast-winner overlap.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: Valid 0DTE charm/pin context where current charm_pin_pullback_long or charm_wall_breakaway_long is exposed to pin-avoid/tail behavior.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Activated dormant profile has positive standalone PnL or lowers combined charm/pin worst MAE, total net profit remains >=98% of leader, protected fast-winner retention >=95%, and no slow trades.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Call-wall acceptance may be a clean high-efficiency option-flow micro-regime.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Looser sample building may admit call-wall chop or simply cannibalize stronger pullback/charm profiles.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Call Wall Acceptance Isolated Sample Builder
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Call-wall acceptance seconds/distance, call-volume expansion, net/forward DEX, gamma score, profile-level trade attribution.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE call-wall acceptance with sustained strike acceptance, positive forward DEX, and call-volume expansion.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: At least 3 total call_wall_acceptance_long trades, positive standalone PnL, PnL/exposure >=250 preferred, worst MAE <= -150, total net profit >=98% of leader.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Deep-heat pullback companion could separate high-PnL pullback flow from tail-heavy low-progress recoveries.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Config-only static gates overlap too many winners; code may be needed for path-progress timing.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Pullback Deep-Heat Companion Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: pullback_long trade paths, early MFE/MAE, setup quality, ES higher-low/high distances, ADX/RSI slopes, option state.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: pullback_long entries with weak early progress, low first-15m MFE, or MFE after MAE despite acceptable entry structure.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Retain >=90% pullback_long PnL while improving pullback worst MAE from -403.2 and keeping total PnL/exposure flat or better.

next_experiments.config_bias: Use a full valid-0DTE router-list activation for exactly one dormant pin-avoid concept, with minimal same-profile tuning only. Do not activate multiple dormant profiles in the same run.
next_experiments.goal: Discover whether dormant pin-avoid routing can split charm/pin tail behavior without broad veto pruning.
next_experiments.hypothesis: Some charm_pin_pullback_long and charm_wall_breakaway_long heat belongs to a separate pin-avoid state that can be attributed by router split rather than by global filters.
next_experiments.name: Dormant Pin-Avoid Attribution Split
next_experiments.primary_metric: Activated profile standalone PnL, combined charm-pin/charm-wall worst MAE, protected fast-winner retention, total risk-adjusted score.
next_experiments.risk: Very small sample and router cannibalization risk; reject if total PnL falls below 98% of leader or protected fast winners are blocked.

next_experiments.config_bias: Keep breakout_long_1dte baseline thresholds stable. Add/activate a one-dte pullback companion only if legal; otherwise leave 1DTE untouched.
next_experiments.goal: Test whether 1DTE-only flow is two regimes: breakout continuation and pullback/reclaim.
next_experiments.hypothesis: Prior direct tightening failed because breakout_long_1dte is already useful; a companion split may improve MAE without reducing the current $1305 1DTE contribution.
next_experiments.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Split
next_experiments.primary_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL, 1DTE average/worst MAE, breakout_long_1dte retained PnL/trades, total score.
next_experiments.risk: Companion may be unreachable or may cannibalize the profitable 20-trade baseline.

next_experiments.config_bias: Build sample only inside call_wall_acceptance_long. Avoid loosening global option-flow or DEX behavior across other profiles.
next_experiments.goal: Determine whether the one clean call-wall acceptance winner is a real profile edge.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The current single trade had excellent efficiency and low MAE, suggesting a narrow call-wall acceptance micro-regime may be under-routed.
next_experiments.name: Call Wall Acceptance Sample Builder
next_experiments.primary_metric: call_wall_acceptance_long trade count, standalone PnL, PnL/exposure, worst MAE, total PnL retention.
next_experiments.risk: One-trade evidence is fragile; broader thresholds may create low-quality chop or steal better pullback/charm entries.

next_experiments.config_bias: Study split-style profile attribution or narrow local guards only. Do not repeat broad setup-quality, RSI-slope, ADX-slope, or ES-VWAP caps that block protected winners.
next_experiments.goal: Separate pullback_long high-PnL core from deep-heat low-progress recoveries.
next_experiments.hypothesis: pullback_long is the largest edge but still tail-heavy; its worst MAE and avg MAE can improve through local state separation rather than global pruning.
next_experiments.name: Pullback Deep-Heat Split Study
next_experiments.primary_metric: pullback_long PnL retention, pullback worst MAE, total PnL/exposure hour, protected winner retention, total score.
next_experiments.risk: Static feature filters overlap profitable winners, especially fast and extended-VWAP trades.

next_experiments.config_bias: Continue only admission/sample-builder studies around supported wall-breakaway geometry. Freeze exit compression and keep the convex target philosophy intact.
next_experiments.goal: Increase charm_wall_breakaway_long sample or PnL while preserving its convex payoff profile.
next_experiments.hypothesis: charm_wall_breakaway_long has small-sample but strong PnL/hour and median MFE; prior exit compression hurt, so the next test should preserve realization and only examine admission geometry.
next_experiments.name: Charm Wall Convex Admission Follow-Through
next_experiments.primary_metric: charm_wall_breakaway_long PnL, trade count, PnL/exposure >=225, total net profit >=98% leader, global worst MAE no worse than -513.4.
next_experiments.risk: Relaxed wall geometry can admit chase entries or cannibalize existing high-quality charm-wall winners.

next_experiments.config_bias: Treat STALE_0DTE separately from EXPIRED_0DTE if possible. Do not disturb VALID_0DTE routing or the single EXPIRED_0DTE winner.
next_experiments.goal: Decide whether stale no-0DTE fallback deserves stricter routing or lower priority.
next_experiments.hypothesis: STALE_0DTE is positive but inefficient, especially breakout_long_no_0dte at $40.5/hour and -250.25 worst MAE; a state-specific holdout may improve risk-adjusted exposure.
next_experiments.name: No-0DTE Data-State Efficiency Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: Fallback PnL/exposure, fallback worst MAE, unchanged VALID_0DTE PnL/trade count, total net profit >=98%, slow_12h = 0.
next_experiments.risk: Only 5 stale trades and 1 expired trade; overfitting can remove useful fallback insurance.

research_notes: Do not emit concrete config changes from this plan. The optimizer should translate these into legal config-only experiments. The main discovery priority is attribution: one dormant router activation at a time, one 1DTE companion test at a time, and isolated sample building for call_wall_acceptance_long. Avoid global slope/quality filters despite their intuitive appeal because counterfactuals show heavy protected-winner loss. Use run_029/run_037/run_040 robustness as the benchmark, not only run_044 raw PnL, because run_044 improved net profit but worsened drawdown and worst MAE.
risk_controls: Reject experiments with total net profit below 98% of the robust leader unless worst MAE improvement is large and deliberate.
Require slow_12h trades to remain 0; any reintroduction of slow tails is a hard warning.
Measure protected fast-winner and extended-VWAP-winner retention explicitly before promoting any entry-pruning or veto-boundary experiment.
Do not optimize dead inactive profiles without a single-profile activation attribution test.
Do not repeat direct breakout_long_1dte tightening; use companion split logic instead.
Freeze charm_wall_breakaway_long exit compression; prior evidence showed it reduced score and net profit.
Treat STALE_0DTE and EXPIRED_0DTE separately because the expired sample is one highly efficient winner while stale breakout fallback is inefficient.
